title: fix: map text provider rate limits to 502
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Removes the text-handler 429 passthrough for upstream provider failures.
- Lets shared upstream remapping return 502 while preserving `upstreamStatus: 429` for debugging.
- Adds regression coverage for text handler responses and Tinybird `error_event` payloads.
- Checks: `biome check --write gen.pollinations.ai/src/text/handler.ts gen.pollinations.ai/test/error-observability.test.ts`; `vitest run test/error-observability.test.ts test/text/genericOpenAIClient.test.ts`.

gist.headline: It's not you, it's them
gist.blurb: We stopped gaslighting your API clients. When our upstream text providers get rate-limited, we now return a 502 instead of a 429.
gist.summary: Upstream provider rate limits in the text handler are now correctly remapped to 502 Bad Gateway errors instead of passing through as 429s, while preserving the original 429 in the `upstreamStatus` field for debugging.
gist.impact: API consumers will no longer confuse upstream provider rate limits with their own client rate limits, allowing for more accurate retry logic.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee fixing a glowing terminal sign, changing a '429' error code to a '502', while the Nomnom creature eats the old error logs.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny yellow hard hat, fixing a glowing retro terminal screen that displays '502' in chunky pixel font. A round tan Nomnom creature sits nearby happily eating a stack of old '429' error logs. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
